name: Pico del Buitre Circular Trail
name_original: Picón del Buitre - Observatorio Astrofísico de Javalambre – circular desde Arcos de las Salinas
description: Hike the difficult 12.5-mile Picón del Buitre circular route from Arcos de las Salinas, gaining 3198 feet to the Astrophysical Observatory.
region_intro: Hiking around Arcos De Las Salinas offers diverse landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain with prominent peaks such as Pico del Buitre and Pico Javalambre. The region features valleys like the Arcos River valley, which includes lush greenery and a flowing river. Dense pine forests are common, through which many hiking trails meander, alongside various barrancos (ravines). Best hiking trails around Arcos De Las Salinas The most popular hiking route is Pico del Buitre Circular Trail, a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route involves a significant ascent to Pico del Buitre, offering expansive panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail offers demanding ascents rewarded with spectacular mountain views of the surrounding sierra. Local hikers also love the The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through dense pine forests, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Arcos De Las Salinas is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Arcos De Las Salinas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Arcos De Las Salinas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails in and around Arcos De Las Salinas, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 160 times.

Q: What kind of landscapes and terrain will I encounter while hiking in Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: Hikers in Arcos De Las Salinas will experience varied terrain, including mountainous areas with prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;, lush river valleys such as the Arcos River valley, and dense pine forests. You'll also find barrancos (ravines) and open fields, providing a rich and diverse outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, Arcos De Las Salinas offers several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976345844/pico-del-buitre-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 20.1 km route with significant ascent. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976332845/javalambre-circular-from-javalambre-station-via-rabada-y-navarro-refuge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.2 km trail offering spectacular mountain views.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hikes in Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: Yes, there are 7 easy trails suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981474858/torrijas-circular-route-on-the-cholesterol-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijas - circular route on the Cholesterol Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.2 km path with minimal elevation change, perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region boasts several natural highlights. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de las Barracas or Cerro Calderón 1838m&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; offers a beautiful waterfall, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dino Route of Aras de los Olmos&lt;/a&gt; is a unique natural monument.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory (Pico del Buitre)&lt;/a&gt; is a significant modern landmark. Historically, the Reales Salinas de Arcos (Royal Salt Pans) are a Bien de Interés Cultural, featuring the 18th-century Ermita de la Virgen de los Dolores and the Iglesia de la Inmaculada Concepción within its complex.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: The best time to hike in Arcos De Las Salinas generally depends on your preferred activity. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for most trails. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter provides opportunities for snow hiking in higher mountainous areas like Pico Javalambre, though conditions can be challenging.

Q: Is Arcos De Las Salinas suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: Many trails in Arcos De Las Salinas are suitable for dogs, allowing you to explore the diverse landscapes with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to tranquil river walks, and the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Pico del Buitre and Pico Javalambre.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking paths that pass through Arcos De Las Salinas?
A: Yes, Arcos De Las Salinas is part of the GR-10, a significant long-distance trekking route. This path connects to various other hiking and mountain biking trails, offering opportunities for extended journeys through the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: Where can I find dense pine forests for hiking?
A: Dense pine forests are a characteristic feature of the region. Many trails meander through these forests, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822450801/the-forest-of-las-blancas-circular-from-puebla-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, offering shaded and scenic routes.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, Arcos De Las Salinas offers 10 difficult routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976345844/pico-del-buitre-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a demanding 20.1 km route with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding challenge and expansive panoramic views.
tips: If possible, mount a light development to climb without overloading it, it is quite a hard climb. Bring water and avoid the hottest hours in summer, because in the upper part there are no shadows

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Picón del Buitre circular hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular hike, covering approximately 20.1 kilometers, generally takes around 6 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration is for active hiking time and does not include breaks for rest or enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You'll encounter a significant, continuous climb with sections of well-paved surfaces (some recently asphalted), unpaved paths, and rocky areas. Expect to traverse ravines (barrancos) and pass through pine forests in lower sections, which give way to more exposed, arid landscapes as you gain elevation. Notable rock formations like 'Los Cuchillos' are also part of the landscape.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is categorized as difficult and requires very good physical fitness. The continuous ascent, steep gradients, and varied terrain make it unsuitable for beginners or families with young children. There are few opportunities for rest during the climb.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory (Pico del Buitre) at the summit, offering amazing panoramic views of the Javalambre massif and beyond. Along the path, you can also discover the source of the Arcos River and unique rock formations. The remote location also means exceptionally dark skies, making it a prime spot for stargazing if you visit at night.

faqs.question: Where can I park and find the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Arcos de las Salinas. You should be able to find parking within the village, which serves as the trailhead for this specific circular route. The ascent initially follows the course of the Arcos River from the village.

faqs.question: Is the Picón del Buitre circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, given the trail's difficult nature, significant elevation gain, and varied terrain including rocky sections and potential for snow at higher altitudes, it's crucial to assess your dog's fitness and endurance. Always keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging paths.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during late spring, summer, and early autumn. During winter, snow can be present at higher altitudes, making the trail more challenging and potentially requiring specialized gear. Summer offers clear skies and warmer temperatures, but be prepared for sun exposure as elevation increases.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for hiking in Sierra de Javalambre?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail or accessing the natural areas within the Sierra de Javalambre. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, pack plenty of water, high-energy snacks, and appropriate layers of clothing for varying weather conditions. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential for the varied terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is crucial, especially at higher, exposed elevations. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with other official trails. You will encounter small sections of the [CIMA TE02] Pico del Buitre, De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa Arcos de las Salinas - Camarena de la Sierra), and VF-TE-02 trails along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near Arcos de las Salinas after the hike?
faqs.answer: Arcos de las Salinas is a small village, and while specific establishments aren't detailed in the trail information, it's common for such villages to have local bars or restaurants where you can find refreshments and a meal after your hike. It's advisable to check local listings or inquire upon arrival.

faqs.question: What makes the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory a notable destination?
faqs.answer: The Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory is a significant scientific facility, renowned for its location in an area with exceptionally low light pollution, making it ideal for astrotourism and stargazing. The challenging climb to the observatory has also gained fame in professional cycling, having been featured as a demanding stage finish in the Vuelta a España multiple times, highlighting its rigorous nature.
